examples: Red granite was commonly used in Thebes for the sarcophagi of high officials.
On view are Roman sarcophagi, often with peaked roofs and looking like marble houses.
Sumptuous friezes and inscriptions cursing anyone tampering the grave decorate many sarcophagi found in the outgrowth.
Their kingdom was the Tekke Peninsula, where cliff tombs and sarcophagi still litter the hills above the Mediterranean.
